result:
All changes are complete and validated. Here's the machine-readable summary.

SUMMARY:
Made the guides index (`/docs/guides`) filterable by operator role — the highest-leverage *visible*, task-oriented navigation improvement available, chosen over Pass 5's two flagged options (an invisible internal refactor and a niche share-link feature). A user can now narrow the 16-guide matrix to just what concerns their role instead of scanning all 16.
- `lib/guides-meta.ts`: added `RolGuia` type + a `roles` field to all 16 guides (derived 1:1 from each guide's existing verified `rol` prose — **no new claims**), plus `FiltroRol`, the `filtrosRol` list (with a contextual note per filter), and the pure helper `guiaCoincideRol`.
- `components/guias-indice.tsx`: converted to a client component with a 3-button role filter (`role="group"`, `aria-pressed`, keyboard focus ring), a live "Mostrando X de 16 guías" counter, and empty-layer suppression; card markup and the single-source-of-truth data flow preserved.
- `content/docs/guides/index.mdx`: intro now advertises the filter.
- `.hermes/overnight/progress.md`: added the Pass 6 entry.

Role mapping result: "Responsable del despliegue" shows 10 guides (hides the 6 provider-only requirement guides); "Proveedor" and "Todos" show all 16.

VALIDATION:
- `npm run build` ✓ — compiles, TypeScript passes (no `ignoreBuildErrors`), 78 static pages (route count unchanged; existing index enhanced).
- Exported `out/docs/guides/index.html` ✓ — renders the 3 filter buttons (aria-pressed: 1 `true` + 2 `false`), the accessible group label `Filtrar guías por rol de operador`, the "Mostrando…" counter with the default note, and all 16 guide cards in the initial `todos` state. Client-side filtering is pure, type-checked logic.
- No new data/claims, no new external links, no broken routes.

NEXT_IDEA:
Now that `lib/guides-meta.ts` is a richer single source (it carries `roles`), close the long-standing Pass 4/5 debt: refactor `GuiaResumen` to take only `n="05"` and derive everything (including related-guide chips via `relacionadas` + `tituloCorto` + `hrefGuia`), removing the inline duplication still present in the 16 guide MDX files. A strong follow-on: make the new filter URL-addressable (e.g., `/docs/guides#rol=desplegador`) so the `Clasificador` result and the itinerarios can deep-link users straight into their pre-filtered guide set, closing the diagnosis→navigation loop.

Note: I did not commit — the overnight harness appears to own commits/pushes (per the runtime logs in git status). Say the word if you'd like me to commit these four files.
